name: Stračinska
ocean: Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea
description: Off the coast of Solta, this dive spot offers clear Mediterranean waters with impressive rocky formations and crevices. Schools of bream and damselfish swirl around the underwater cliffs, while octopuses hide in the cracks. The gentle slope makes it ideal for beginners, yet deeper sections attract experienced divers with occasional groupers and moray eels. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters, and the site stays sheltered from strong currents. Colorful sponges and algae cover the rocks, creating a vibrant backdrop. Ideal for photographers and those who enjoy exploring nooks teeming with marine life.

name: Kukuljari
ocean: Europe, Croatia, šibenik-knin, Murter
description: This dive site near Murter features a stunning underwater pinnacle rising from 30 meters to just 5 meters below the surface. Vibrant soft corals and sponges cover the rock, attracting schools of damselfish, groupers, and occasional barracuda. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich waters, creating excellent visibility and thriving marine life. The site offers dramatic drop-offs and swim-throughs for advanced divers, while sheltered areas suit beginners. Watch for octopus hiding in crevices and sea turtles passing by during summer months. Water temperatures range from 14°C in winter to 24°C in summer.

name: Brioni Wreck
ocean: Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea, Adriatic Sea
description: This wreck lies off Vis Island in Croatia’s Adriatic Sea, resting at 28 meters. The intact cargo ship, covered in soft corals, attracts schools of damselfish, groupers, and occasional barracuda. Strong currents bring excellent visibility, making it ideal for advanced divers. The hull offers swim-throughs, while the deck reveals wartime artifacts. Nearby reefs add to the dive’s diversity. Best explored in calm summer conditions.

Expect visibility around 15-20 meters with mild currents, perfect for novice divers. The rocky bottom transitions to sandy patches where stingrays glide. Dive here for relaxed exploration and marine encounters just meters from shore.

name: Kostrena Dive Center House Reef
ocean: Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea, Adriatic Sea
description: This shallow reef near Rijeka offers easy access with diverse marine life. Expect octopus, scorpionfish, and colorful nudibranchs among the boulders and seagrass. Gentle slopes and good visibility make it ideal for beginners. The sheltered bay provides calm conditions year-round. Artificial structures attract schooling fish, adding interest to the rocky bottom. Night dives reveal crabs and other nocturnal creatures. Water temperatures range from 12°C in winter to 24°C in summer.

Ideal for all skill levels, the site provides gentle currents and a rich ecosystem to explore. The reef’s shallow sections are perfect for beginners, while deeper areas reward experienced divers with hidden crevices and overhangs. A great spot for underwater photography.

name: B-17G Wreck
ocean: Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea, Adriatic Sea
description: Off the coast of Vis Island lies a well-preserved B-17G bomber wreck resting at 50 meters. The intact fuselage and wings attract schools of damselfish and groupers. Strong currents and depth make this a challenging dive, best for advanced divers. The wreck's history and marine life create a haunting yet fascinating underwater scene. Visibility is often excellent, revealing details like the bomber's turret and propellers.
